# US Defense & AI National Security ETF Portfolio

> A structured investment portfolio covering US traditional defense primes and next-generation defense-AI companies, built around the thesis of a global rearmament supercycle. Part of the Investment Idea Column series in the vibe-investing repository, as of 2026-05-25. Informational only — not investment advice. Available in Korean and English.

This document analyzes the structural investment opportunity created by record-high global defense spending (projected >$3T in 2026, $3.7T by 2030) driven by the Ukraine war, the Iran–Israel conflict, German and Japanese rearmament, and NATO's 5%-of-GDP commitment. It recommends a barbell strategy combining traditional defense primes with defense-AI growth names, accessed efficiently through ETFs. All return and dividend figures are estimates as of May 2026 and vary by source.

## Core Beneficiaries (Traditional Defense Primes)

- [Lockheed Martin (LMT)]: ~+75% 3-yr cumulative total return; ~2.6% forward dividend yield; F-35 (296-jet, $12.5B contract) and PAC-3 missile backlog at record high; 24 consecutive years of dividend growth.
- [RTX / Raytheon (RTX)]: ~+90% 3-yr cumulative total return; ~1.4% forward dividend yield; Patriot and radar global exports; direct beneficiary of the Golden Dome project.
- [Northrop Grumman (NOC)]: ~+55% 3-yr cumulative total return; ~1.4% forward dividend yield; leader in B-21 stealth bomber and UAVs; stable profitability.

## Defense AI Trailblazers

- [Palantir (PLTR)]: Public; ~+1,000% 3-yr return; no dividend; military-AI data integration and decision support; won the US Army TITAN project; flagship publicly traded defense-AI name.
- [Anduril]: Private (not directly investable by retail); autonomous unmanned systems leader; 'Fury' unmanned fighter; leads USAF Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA) program.
- [Shield AI]: Private (not directly investable by retail); 'Hivemind' AI pilot for all military aircraft; autonomous flight innovator.
- Key caveat: Anduril and Shield AI are private; retail exposure is only possible indirectly through ETFs (e.g., SHLD) or secondary markets.

## Defense ETFs (Diversification Vehicles)

- [Global X Defense Tech (SHLD)]: ~+44%/yr since Sep-2023 launch (partial 3-yr data); +48–101% trailing 1-yr; 0.50% expense ratio; concentrated in next-gen defense tech/AI (Palantir, Rheinmetall); highest growth potential.
- [iShares US Aerospace & Defense (ITA)]: ~+20–25%/yr 3-yr annualized; +38% trailing 1-yr; 0.38% expense ratio; large-cap aerospace/defense (GE, LMT, RTX); ~$16B AUM (largest); stability-first.
- [Invesco Aerospace & Defense (PPA)]: ~+29–30%/yr 3-yr annualized; +38% trailing 1-yr; 0.58% expense ratio; 60 holdings; strong diversified performer.
- [TIGER US Defense TOP10 (494840.KS)]: Korea-listed; usable in pension/ISA accounts; no FX conversion; top-10 US defense stocks; tax-efficient for Korean investors.

## Model Portfolios (defense-sleeve = 100%; suggested 5–15% of total assets)

- Conservative (income + stability): ITA 45%, SHLD 15%, PPA 15%, LMT 15%, RTX/NOC 10%.
- Balanced (core-satellite): ITA 35%, SHLD 30%, PPA 15%, LMT 10%, PLTR 10%.
- Aggressive (growth-focused): SHLD 45%, ITA 20%, PPA 10%, LMT 5%, PLTR 20%.

## Risk Factors

- Geopolitical de-escalation: ceasefires/peace deals could trigger sharp declines; manage entry cost via staggered buying.
- Government budget volatility: US federal budget, continuing resolutions, administration changes may delay orders; LMT/RTX backlogs are relatively defensive.
- FX (KRW/USD): KRW-denominated returns exposed to currency moves; mitigate with Korea-listed or FX-hedged products.
- Valuation / concentration: PLTR and SHLD carry high valuations; mitigate single-theme risk via ETF diversification.
- Private-stock access limits: Anduril and Shield AI accessible only indirectly via ETFs.
